
### exercise  {.smaller}


```
char *array;
// PRIME
posix_memalign(&array, CACHE_SIZE, CACHE_SIZE);
AccessAllOf(array);
// (some code we don't control)
other_array[mystery * BLOCK_SIZE] += 1;
// PROBE
for (int i = 0; i < CACHE_SIZE; i += BLOCK_SIZE) {
    if (CheckIfSlowToAccess(&array[i])) {
    ...
    }
}

```
 

* 64KB ($2^{16}$B) direct-mapped cache with 64B blocks
* array[0x800] slow to access;
* other_array at <code>0x4000000</code>
* value of <code>mystery</code>?


### exercise solution (1)  {.smaller}


* NUM_SETS = 64KB/64B = 1K (1024) sets
* array[0x800] has cache index [ <code>0x800</code>/BLOCK_SIZE mod NUM_SETS]{.my-small} 

   * = cache index 32

* know <code>[]{.my-small} other_array[mystery * BLOCK_SIZE]</code> had same index 
<hr class="vspace" />
* <code>other_array[0]</code> at cache index 0 

   * (0x4000000 / BLOCK_SIZE) mod NUM_SETS = 0



### exercise solution (2)  {.smaller}


* recall have found: 

   * <code>other_array[0]</code> at index 0;
   * <code>other_array[mystery*BLOCK_SIZE]</code> has index 32 (same as <code>array[0x800]</code>)

* <code>other_array[X]</code> at cache index (0 + X/BLOCK_SIZE  mod NUM_SETS) 

   * advanced by X/BLOCK_SIZE blocks
   * wrapping around after NUM_SETS blocks
 
<hr class="vspace" />
* X = mystery * BLOCK_SIZE
* 32 = 0 + mystery mod NUM_SETS
* mystery = 32 or 32 $\pm$ 1024 or 32 $\pm$ 1024 $\times$ 2 or etc.
